title: Process Selection (Manufacturing)
description: Assesses analytical and critical understanding of manufacturing process selection, process characteristics, product–process matrix, process analysis, break-even analysis, and business process reengineering.
subject: DSC2370 - Operations Management

questions.question: Why is process selection considered a strategic decision rather than an operational one?
options: Because it focuses only on daily scheduling decisions
Because it determines the long-term cost structure and flexibility of the operation
Because it is reversible without major investment
Because it affects marketing decisions only
questions.answer: Because it determines the long-term cost structure and flexibility of the operation

questions.question: Which combination of process characteristics is MOST likely to result in the lowest unit processing cost?
options: Low volume, high variety, high visibility
High volume, low variety, low visibility
Low volume, low variety, high variation
questions.answer: High volume, low variety, low visibility

questions.question: In a project process, which feature creates the greatest management challenge?
options: Uniqueness and complexity of each output
questions.answer: Uniqueness and complexity of each output

questions.question: Which statement best differentiates a jobbing process from a batch process?
options: Jobbing processes have higher volumes than batch processes
Batch processes involve greater customization than jobbing processes
Batch processes produce repeated outputs in identifiable lots
Jobbing processes rely on dedicated assembly lines
questions.answer: Batch processes produce repeated outputs in identifiable lots

questions.question: Why are mass processes positioned between batch and continuous processes on the volume–variety continuum?
options: They combine standardized products with discrete output
questions.answer: They combine standardized products with discrete output

questions.question: Which characteristic is UNIQUE to continuous flow processes compared to mass processes?
options: Uninterrupted material flow over long periods
questions.answer: Uninterrupted material flow over long periods

questions.question: The product–process matrix primarily helps managers to:
options: Align product volume–variety with appropriate process structures
questions.answer: Align product volume–variety with appropriate process structures

questions.question: Operating far from the diagonal of the product–process matrix is MOST likely to result in:
Which of the following is NOT a primary purpose of a process flowchart?
High work-in-process inventory usually indicates:
options: Presence of bottlenecks or long cycle times
questions.answer: Presence of bottlenecks or long cycle times

questions.question: According to Little’s Law, throughput time will increase if:
options: Work-in-process increases, holding cycle time constant
questions.answer: Work-in-process increases, holding cycle time constant

questions.question: Which document is MOST useful for understanding the sequence of manufacturing operations?
Break-even analysis is especially valuable in process selection because it:
options: Links cost structure with expected demand volume
Ensures maximum profit at all volumes
questions.answer: Links cost structure with expected demand volume

questions.question: A process with high fixed costs and low variable costs is MOST suitable when:
options: Demand volume is expected to be high
Product life cycle is very short
questions.answer: Demand volume is expected to be high

questions.question: The point of indifference between two processes represents:
options: The demand level where total costs are equal
The maximum capacity of a process
questions.answer: The demand level where total costs are equal

questions.question: A batch process always has lower unit costs than a mass process.
High customer contact generally increases processing costs.
Continuous processes are suitable for products with frequent design changes.
Processes that handle low volume and high variety are typically positioned at the ________ end of the volume–variety continuum.
The mathematical relationship linking throughput time, work-in-process, and cycle time is known as ________ Law.
Which factors increase process complexity?
Which process types typically produce to stock?
Match the process type with its typical application.
Match each document with its purpose.
Explain why product design and process design should not be treated as independent decisions.
Why does moving from a job process to a mass process usually reduce unit costs?
Briefly explain how bottlenecks affect throughput time.
Why might a firm deliberately choose a process with higher unit costs?
Explain one risk associated with heavy automation in manufacturing processes.
What distinguishes business process reengineering from continuous improvement?
Critically analyze how the volume–variety relationship influences the choice between jobbing, batch, mass, and continuous processes. Use examples to support your answer.
Evaluate the role of break-even analysis in selecting among alternative manufacturing processes. Discuss its strengths and limitations in real-world decision making.
